content.problemStatement:
Given a string containing lowercase English letters and parentheses '(' and ')', find a valid parentheses string by removing the minimum number of parentheses. If no valid string can be formed except empty string, print -1. A string is valid if:


It contains only lowercase letters, or
It can be written as (A) where A is valid, or
It can be written as AB where A and B are valid

Input Format

First line contains TTT, the number of test cases
Next TTT lines contain string SSS


Output Format

For each test case, print the valid string after removing minimum parentheses. If only empty string is possible, print -1.
constraints: S contains only lowercase letters, '(' and ')'
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string S consisting of lowercase English letters and the characters '(' and ')'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the resulting valid string after removing the minimum number of parentheses. If the only possible result is an empty string, print -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: remove the first ')' → ab(c)d.
Test case 2: all parentheses are unmatched → -1.
Test case 3: remove the first '(' → a(b(c)d).
Test case 4: already valid.
In the first string the trailing ')' is unmatched and removed. The second string contains no parentheses, so it stays unchanged.
First string: every parenthesis is unmatched, result is empty → -1. Second string is already a correct pair.

solution.approach: We scan the string from left to right using a stack that stores indices of unmatched '(' characters. When we encounter a ')', if the stack is non‑empty we pop one '(' (they form a matched pair); otherwise the ')' is unmatched and we mark it for deletion. After the scan, any indices left in the stack correspond to unmatched '(' and are also marked for deletion. Finally we build the answer by skipping all marked characters. If the resulting string is empty we output -1. This greedy removal deletes the minimum possible number of parentheses because every deletion is forced by an unmatched parenthesis; all matched pairs are kept.
solution.timeComplexity: O(|S|) per test case, O(total length of input) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(|S|) for the stack and the output string (both linear in the length of the input string).
